coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in is likely about whether a senior engineer’s current commitments, stakeholder updates, and interrupt load still support the team’s real priorities.
openingQuestion: What have you been saying yes and no to over the last two weeks, and where is that helping or hurting the work that matters most?
listenFor: whether they name one concrete task or request they recently declined, deferred, or absorbed
if they point to a specific cross-team dependency, owner, or pending decision that is slowing delivery
if they cite a recent stakeholder update, meeting, or message and describe what decision or response it triggered
avoid: do not turn the check-in into a status readout of every task
do not jump straight to fixing workload before you know what they are choosing versus inheriting
goodOutcome: You and Test have agreed which work a senior engineer should actively own now, what can be deprioritized, and one dependency or stakeholder communication you will help unblock.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one current project and one recent interrupt or side request so you can test whether their time is aligned to the team’s top bets.
